backend/main.mo: Motoko actor managing per-player game state (health, ammunition, score, enemies defeated) with functions to start games, take damage, recharge weapons, increase scores, defeat enemies, and query current state.
feature_evidence.json: Feature evidence report documenting five error-handling and initialization features with associated files, components, and test IDs for deployment load error tracking, dependency verification, fallback rendering, retry logic, and error boundary implementation.
frontend-file-summaries.txt: Pre-existing summaries of frontend TypeScript/React files covering hooks, components, and configuration for the 3D shooter game with error handling, lifecycle management, and UI elements.
frontend/index.css: Tailwind CSS configuration with OKLCH color system defining light and dark theme CSS variables for a dark, atmospheric game aesthetic.
frontend/index.html: Minimal HTML entry point with viewport meta tag, title 'Timeless', and script loading the React app from main.tsx.
frontend/src/App.tsx: Root React component wrapping the Game component in an ErrorBoundary with lifecycle logging of mount/unmount events, URL, user agent, and screen dimensions.
frontend/src/components/Arena.tsx: 3D arena component rendering floor, skybox sphere, walls, obstacles, and enemy entities using react-three/cannon physics with error handling and texture disposal on unmount.
frontend/src/components/CanvasErrorFallb: Fallback UI component displaying user-friendly error message with troubleshooting tips and retry/reload buttons when 3D canvas initialization fails.
frontend/src/components/Enemy.tsx: Enemy entity component rendering a 3D box with health bar, color-coded damage states, idle rotation animation, and physics body using react-three/cannon.
frontend/src/components/ErrorBoundary.ts: React error boundary component that catches and displays application errors with detailed stack traces, timestamps, and a reset/reload button. Releases pointer lock before reloading the page.
frontend/src/components/Game.tsx: Main game component that orchestrates the 3D canvas, physics, player, arena, enemies, and HUD. Manages pointer lock, game initialization with retry logic, and coordinates shooting/enemy hit events.
frontend/src/components/HealthBar.tsx: Simple health bar component that displays current health as a percentage-based filled bar with a background container.
frontend/src/components/HUD.tsx: Heads-up display component showing crosshair, score, health bar, ammunition count, and footer with attribution link. Renders UI overlays on top of the game canvas.
frontend/src/components/LoadingScreen.ts: Loading screen overlay with spinner, customizable message, and optional retry attempt counter. Uses backdrop blur and centers content over the game.
frontend/src/components/MuzzleFlash.tsx: 3D muzzle flash effect component that displays a textured plane in front of the camera with additive blending and fading opacity animation when the weapon fires.
frontend/src/components/Player.tsx: Player controller component managing first-person camera, physics sphere collision, mouse-look rotation, WASD movement, and raycasting for shooting enemies. Handles click events to fire weapon and detect hits.
frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts: React Query hook for managing the backend actor instance with Internet Identity authentication. Creates anonymous or authenticated actors and invalidates dependent queries when identity changes.
frontend/src/hooks/useDependencyCheck.ts: Hook that checks for WebGL support and verifies that Three.js, React Three Fiber, and Cannon physics libraries are loaded. Returns readiness status, loading state, and detailed dependency information.
frontend/src/hooks/useEnemies.ts: Manages enemy state with initial positions, health tracking, and damage handling. Provides damageEnemy callback to reduce enemy health and mark them as destroyed when health reaches zero.
frontend/src/hooks/useGameInitialization: Handles game initialization lifecycle with retry logic (max 3 attempts) and state tracking. Provides methods to mark initialization success/failure and reset state.
frontend/src/hooks/useGameState.ts: Manages local game state (health, ammo, score, enemies defeated) and syncs with backend via useBackendGameState hook. Provides methods to initialize game, decrement ammo, handle enemy defeats, and take damage.
frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.t: Provides Internet Identity authentication context and hooks for ICP login/logout. Creates AuthClient with configurable options and manages identity state throughout the authentication lifecycle.
frontend/src/hooks/usePlayerControls.ts: Tracks keyboard (WASD, Space) and mouse movement input for player controls. Returns current control state and mouse movement delta with proper cleanup on unmount.
frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts: Provides React Query hooks for backend game state management with mutations for startGame, takeDamage, defeatEnemy, increaseScore, and rechargeWeapon. Uses useActor hook to interact with the canister.
frontend/src/hooks/useWeapon.ts: Manages weapon firing state with rate limiting and muzzle flash effects. Returns fire callback that enforces fire rate cooldown and triggers temporary muzzle flash display.
frontend/src/index.css: Tailwind CSS configuration with custom CSS variables for theming (colors, radius, charts) in both light and dark modes using OKLCH color space.
frontend/src/main.tsx: Application entry point that sets up React root with QueryClientProvider for data fetching and InternetIdentityProvider for ICP authentication, then renders the App component.
frontend/src/styles/game-theme.css: Defines CSS custom properties and classes for game UI styling including HUD elements (crosshair, health bar, ammo counter), cards, buttons, and a dark blue/purple color scheme using oklch color space.
frontend/tailwind.config.js: Tailwind CSS configuration extending the default theme with custom colors using oklch CSS variables, typography, container queries, animations, and custom font families for the game UI.
project_state.json: Project state tracking document for a 3D first-person shooter game built with React Three Fiber, listing implemented features (player movement, shooting mechanics, HUD, game state) and known issues with game loading and deployment.
